briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis: A Threat and Warning Becoming Reality
sections.content: Europe is experiencing its worst heatwave, with temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius despite it being June, warning that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern [1]. This sense of crisis has even led to the publication of poetry collections documenting climate change, demonstrating a widespread awareness of the climate crisis across our society [8]. While some still raise questions about the reality of climate change [17], the majority of opinions emphasize that climate change is an undeniable reality, urging heightened vigilance [18]. Reports that even birds in nature are sending warning messages about climate change [19] represent the suffering experienced by the entire ecosystem and demand immediate action from us.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Action: Progress in Local Communities and Policy
sections.content: Domestically, diverse efforts to address the climate crisis are actively underway at both community and policy levels. The case of a city with a petrochemical industrial complex transforming into a climate-leading city through 10 years of public-private-industry-academia cooperation [2, 3] demonstrates the possibility of coexistence between industry and environment. Furthermore, Gangjin-gun has completed the creation of urban forests to respond to climate change [4, 5], Jeongeup Office of Education is operating carbon-neutral ecological education training [6], and Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province is expanding wooden culture experience centers to broaden spaces for carbon-neutral practices [7], thereby focusing on education and infrastructure expansion. The passage of an amendment for the establishment of a Climate Crisis Response Committee in the Daegu City Council [10] raises expectations for systematic climate policy development at the local government level, and a discussion is also scheduled in the National Assembly to explore ways to achieve carbon neutrality through eco-friendly railways [11]. As such, with the participation of local residents (e.g., Gangseo 2-dong Saemaul Women's Association participating in carbon-neutral practice classes [9]) and field learning by educational institutions (e.g., Daegu Health College Department of Radiology students' on-site education on renewable energy [15]), domestic climate action is steadily gaining depth.

sections.title: 🌱 Efforts to Achieve Carbon Neutrality Through Innovation
sections.content: Innovative technological and institutional efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals are also steadily progressing. AMEXG Group and Eco-friendly World Co., Ltd. are building a kenaf-based carbon credit Web3 ecosystem [12], exploring carbon reduction methods utilizing new technologies. Notably, in 2026, the Carbon Neutrality Point system will be reorganized and expanded, offering points such as 3,000 won for tree-planting activities and 100 won for group purchases, thereby actively encouraging eco-friendly consumption [13]. Meanwhile, in the UK, Good Energy has been named the best green energy supplier [14], indicating a continuous global expansion of interest and investment in eco-friendly energy solutions.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Research and Response Efforts
sections.content: The international community continues its research and practical response efforts to understand the past of climate change and adapt to the future. A study in Israel revealed 4,000 years of climate change and human adaptation secrets [16], providing insights into climate change from a long-term perspective. Furthermore, one study revealed that extensive coral reef areas have the potential to resist climate change [20], offering hope for ecosystem resilience. Interestingly, a coastal village showcased a unique way to combat climate change through a boat race [21], presenting a creative method for local communities to respond to the climate crisis.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis manifesting across the globe and the diverse domestic and international efforts to combat it. We hope these efforts will continue, leading to a more sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
